items.headline: Colorado Avalanche showcase scoring depth in Game 4 win
items.description:
ST. PAUL, Minn. -- The Colorado Avalanche's response started with a center-ice face-off win by Nazem Kadri in the aftermath of Nico Sturm's game-tying goal midway through the third period.
What followed were three big-boy shifts capped by a goal from Parker Kelly, the game-winner in a 5-2 victory against

items.headline: Colorado Avalanche Minnesota Wild Game 4 recap May 11 2026
items.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. -- Parker Kelly scored his first career Stanley Cup Playoff goal in the third period, and the Colorado Avalanche defeated the Minnesota Wild 5-2 in Game 4 of the Western Conference Second Round at Grand Casino Arena on Monday.​

items.headline: Artturi Lehkonen Sam Malinski out for Colorado Avalanche in Game 4
items.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. -- Artturi Lehkonen and Sam Malinski did not play for the Colorado Avalanche in a 5-2 win against the Minnesota Wild in Game 4 of the Western Conference Second Round at Grand Casino Arena on Monday.

items.headline: 2026 Stanley Cup Playoffs second round schedule television results
items.description: The second round of the 2026 Stanley Cup Playoffs began Saturday with the Carolina Hurricanes defeating the Philadelphia Flyers 3-0. The second round continued on Sunday with the Colorado Avalanche defeating the Minnesota Wild 9-6.

items.headline: Anaheim Ducks still aiming high entering Game 5 against Golden Knights
items.description:
LAS VEGAS -- From the outside, it might seem like the Anaheim Ducks already have exceeded their own expectations in the Stanley Cup Playoffs.
On the inside, however, Anaheim always had high hopes for success in its first postseason appearance in eight years.
“I think we’re playing with house money

items.headline: Golden Knights relying on experience heading into Game 5 against Ducks
items.description:
LAS VEGAS -- The Vegas Golden Knights are well aware of the pressures and emotions that develop as a Stanley Cup Playoff series deepens.
"They've been through so much as a team," Vegas coach John Tortorella said on Monday. "They've gone through all the processes, all the things you go

items.headline: MacKenzie Blackwood to start for Colorado in Game 4 vs Minnesota
items.description:
Mackenzie Blackwood got the start for the Colorado Avalanche in Game 4 of the Western Conference Second Round against the Minnesota Wild at Grand Casino Arena in St. Paul, Minnesota, on Monday.
It is Blackwood’s first start of the 2026 Stanley Cup Playoffs.
Blackwood stopped 12 of 13 shots in

items.headline: Montreal Canadiens coach Martin St. Louis embracing playoff pressure
items.description:
MONTREAL -- In a hockey-crazed city that has taken its passion for the Montreal Canadiens to yet another level here at Stanley Cup Playoff time, Martin St. Louis is a rock star.
And he’s embracing every precious moment of it.

items.headline: Buffalo has 'got to be a lot better' in Game 4 vs Montreal
items.description:
MONTREAL -- There is no blip anymore.
There is no one-game sample size, one game in which the play of the Buffalo Sabres dipped, ready for them to bounce back and recover. They have played two straight games in which they were disappointed by their performance and overwhelmed by the

items.headline: Matthew Schaefer helps Northwell Health open new care center
items.description:
Matthew Schaefer announced on Monday that he will be partnering with New York’s largest health system, Northwell Health, to support cancer patients and their families.
With the help of a $150,000 donation from the Islanders Children’s Foundation, the hospital will open the “Jennifer Schaefer Child Support Center” in honor of

items.headline: Mark Jankowski signs 2 year contract with Carolina Hurricanes
items.description:
Mark Jankowski signed a two-year, $3.7 million contract with the Carolina Hurricanes on Monday. It has an average annual value of $1.85 million,
The 31-year-old forward had 21 points (11 goals, 10 assists) in 68 regular-season games this season. He has one assist in eight Stanley Cup Playoff games for

items.headline: Arber Xhekaj fined for roughing in Canadiens-Sabres Game 3
items.description: NEW YORK -- Montreal Canadiens defenseman Arber Xhekaj has been fined $3,385.42, the maximum allowable under the Collective Bargaining Agreement, for roughing Buffalo Sabres forward Sam Carrick during Game 3 of the teams’ Second Round series in Montreal on Sunday, May 10, the National Hockey League’s Department of Player Safety

items.headline: Beck Malenstyn fined maximum for goalie interference in Game 3
items.description: NEW YORK -- Buffalo Sabres forward Beck Malenstyn has been fined $3,515.63, the maximum allowable under the Collective Bargaining Agreement, for interference against Montreal Canadiens goaltender Jakub Dobes during Game 3 of the teams’ Second Round series in Montreal on Sunday, May 10, the National Hockey League’s Department of Player
